svg.text: The 7 matches that wire an Istio-routed Kubernetes app together
^ not in your YAML — installed by istioctl/operator
The 7 matches — what fails when each one breaks
Within Deployment. Mismatch → kubectl apply REJECTS the file. (loud failure)
Cross-file. Mismatch → Service has 0 endpoints. Traffic silently drops. (silent failure)
Service targetPort ↔ Pod containerPort (app's listening port)
Cross-file. Mismatch → Service forwards to a dead port. Connection refused. (silent failure)
Cross-file. Mismatch → Envoy returns 503 NR (no route). Cluster-not-found in proxy logs.
Cross-file. Mismatch → Envoy cannot reach Service port. 503 UH (no healthy upstream).
VirtualService gateways[] ↔ Gateway metadata.name — ISTIO NEW
Cross-file. Mismatch → VS rules are never applied. Route 404s at edge. Hardest to diagnose.
Gateway selector ↔ istio-ingressgateway Pod labels — ISTIO NEW
Cross-cluster. Mismatch → no edge Envoy applies the Gateway. Port never opens. Total silent failure.
The Istio chain — read top to bottom
│ selector says: this is my Envoy
Two new Istio matches (F, G) connect the
The other five (A–E) are unchanged from
